canonical_definition: LongBench-E is consistently used in the provided literature as a benchmark for evaluating model capabilities on long-context tasks. The prevailing description characterizes it as a collection of 13 real-world applications, with coding and few-shot learning cited as specific examples. A less frequent but more specific definition identifies it as the English-language subset of the broader LongBench benchmark. Across the surveyed work, its primary function is to measure `Long-context processing`, a use case mentioned in multiple papers. One paper also explicitly uses it to assess `Few-shot learning`. The benchmark is described as being "representative of many real-world long-context applications" ("LongMamba: Enhancing Mamba's Long-Context Capabilities via Training-Free Receptive Field Enlargement"), and researchers report conducting experiments on it to compare different models.
papers: DeciMamba: Exploring the Length Extrapolation Potential of Mamba
LongMamba: Enhancing Mamba's Long-Context Capabilities via Training-Free Receptive Field Enlargement
Core Context Aware Transformers for Long Context Language Modeling
Curse of High Dimensionality Issue in Transformer for Long Context Modeling
Long-Short Alignment for Effective Long-Context Modeling in LLMs

definitions.text: A benchmark covering 13 real-world long-context applications including coding, question answering, and few-shot learning.
